troparion.text: As a disciple and companion of Paul / you received in your soul the grace of preaching. / You shone in the night of error and struggled for the Lord’s glory. / Sacred minister of the Savior, Aquila, we all acclaim you.

kontakion.text: The Church has acquired you, O Aquila, / who shine like a great sun. / With the splendor of your teaching / she enlightens those who honor you with faith, / glorious Apostle of the Lord.

saints.name: Venerable Stephen, Abbot of Makhrishche, Vologda

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Stephen, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

Your holy martyr Justus, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Justus;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Hellius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Onesimus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

saints.name: Repose of Venerable Nikodemos the Hagiorite

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “O Victorious Leader...”)
The Orthodox Church honors you as a virtuous member,  / its finest initiate, and a God-bearing teacher of piety; /  for having received heavenly grace, /  you shone your inspirational writings upon those who cry to you: /  “Rejoice, O Father Νikόdēmos.”
